mechanism_narrative: A sequential decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity multiples, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5 percent, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52 percent over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and real GDP growth indicate a supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 40 percent of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained federal funds rate above 4.5 percent restricts credit availability and elevates discount rates, systematically depressing equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of observations where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5 percent, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals a sharp contraction in consumer confidence and construction activity, which transmits to broader equity weakness over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances following a HOUST decline exceeding 10 percent, or the signal's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in payroll employment and growth in real consumer spending indicates robust aggregate demand, which expands corporate profit expectations and lifts equity prices over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 45 percent of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in rising INDPRO periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.5 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations become unanchored, prompting equity risk premium expansion and negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL annualized growth exceeds 3.5 percent, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50 percent over a 5-year sample.
